(born September 30, 1953) is a Canadian-American [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") and [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy") author who was born in France. Stirling is the author of the [Draka series](/wiki/The_Domination "The Domination") of [alternate history](/wiki/Alternate_history "Alternate history") novels, the later [time travel](/wiki/Time_travel "Time travel")/alternate history [Nantucket series](/wiki/Nantucket_series "Nantucket series") and the [Emberverse series](/wiki/The_Emberverse_series "The Emberverse series").

["In Memoriam: Jan Stirling"](https://www.sfwa.org/2021/05/10/in-memoriam-jan-stirling/). [Science Fiction Writers of America](/wiki/Science_Fiction_Writers_of_America "Science Fiction Writers of America"). 10 May 2021. Retrieved 19 July 2025.

Munger, Kel (June 2, 2009). ["6/2/09 Book log"](http://www.newsreview.com/sacramento/bibliolatry/blogs?date=2009-06-01). *[Sacramento News and Review](/wiki/Sacramento_News_and_Review "Sacramento News and Review")*. Retrieved 12 July 2009.

Canadian-American science fiction and fantasy author (born 1953)

| S. M. Stirling | |

| Occupation | Writer |

**Stephen Michael Stirling** (born September 30, 1953) is a Canadian-American [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") and [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy "Fantasy") author who was born in France. Stirling is the author of the [Draka series](/wiki/The_Domination "The Domination") of [alternate history](/wiki/Alternate_history "Alternate history") novels, the later [time travel](/wiki/Time_travel "Time travel")/alternate history [Nantucket series](/wiki/Nantucket_series "Nantucket series") and the [Emberverse series](/wiki/The_Emberverse_series "The Emberverse series").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=S._M._Stirling&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Stirling was born on September 30, 1953, in [Metz](/wiki/Metz "Metz"), France—then the site of a [Royal Canadian Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Canadian_Air_Force "Royal Canadian Air Force") base—to an English mother and Canadian father. He has lived in several countries and currently resides in the United States in [New Mexico](/wiki/New_Mexico "New Mexico").

Stirling, along with [Eric Flint](/wiki/Eric_Flint "Eric Flint"), was [tuckerized](/wiki/Tuckerization "Tuckerization") as a [Secret Service](/wiki/United_States_Secret_Service "United States Secret Service") agent in [John Birmingham](/wiki/John_Birmingham "John Birmingham")'s [alternate history](/wiki/Alternate_history "Alternate history") [WWII](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") novel [*Weapons of Choice*](/wiki/Weapons_of_Choice_(book) "Weapons of Choice (book)") (2004).

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=S._M._Stirling&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Stirling's novels are generally conflict-driven and often describe military situations and [militaristic](/wiki/Militarism "Militarism") cultures. In addition to his books' military, adventure, and exploration focus, he often describes societies with cultural values significantly different from modern Western views. One of his recurring topics is the influence of the culture on an individual's outlook and values, with an emphasis on the idea that most people and societies consider themselves moral.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Stirling frequently explores technological development within the context of many of his novels. The Draka, for instance, choose and face a different imperative in their conquest of Africa, and turn earlier to [breech-loading](/wiki/Breechloader "Breechloader") firearms and steam power than the rest of the Western world. The stranded islanders of the Nantucket series try to rebuild their technological base once the island is thrown back in time to 1250 BC, while the survivors of the "Event" now face a world where electricity, firearms, and internal combustion no longer work.

Stirling also tends to write strong female characters who have prominent roles within the story.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In the past, he has frequently collaborated with other authors, including [David Drake](/wiki/David_Drake "David Drake"), [Jerry Pournelle](/wiki/Jerry_Pournelle "Jerry Pournelle"), [Anne McCaffrey](/wiki/Anne_McCaffrey "Anne McCaffrey"), and [Raymond E. Feist](/wiki/Raymond_E._Feist "Raymond E. Feist").
